events.text: Cynthia Jones, 58-year-old Other female, Current smoker (0.5 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Invasive ductal carcinoma of the Left breast. Clinical staging: T3N2M1, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C50.911. Stage IV HER2-positive breast cancer with liver and bone metastases.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Bone scan performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.

Initiated first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade: Docetaxel 75 mg/m2 + Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w x 6 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated maintenance HER2-directed therapy: Trastuzumab 6 mg/kg + Pertuzumab 420 mg q3w maintenance. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Bone scan performed for restaging after maintenance HER2-directed therapy.
Clinical visit for results review after maintenance HER2-directed therapy.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated second-line HER2-directed ADC: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) 5.4 mg/kg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 alopecia. Counseled on expected hair regrowth post-treatment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after second-line HER2-directed ADC.
Clinical visit for results review after second-line HER2-directed ADC.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Cynthia Jones
MRN: patient_2d12d8b2603e
Specimen ID: S2023-16402
Date of Procedure: 2023-01-08
Date of Report: 2023-01-11

SPECIMEN: Left breast, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Invasive ductal carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
ER: Negative (0%)
PR: Negative (0%)
HER2 IHC: 3+ (positive)
Ki-67: 45%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Cynthia Jones - Left breast" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 4.2 x 2.9 x 2.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Cynthia Jones
MRN: patient_2d12d8b2603e
Date of Study: 2023-01-10

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
Multiple foci of increased radiotracer uptake in the axial and appendicular skeleton consistent with widespread osseous metastatic disease. Approximately 5 lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions as described.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Cynthia Jones
MRN: patient_2d12d8b2603e
Date: 2023-01-15
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Jones is a 58-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, IV. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage IV HER2-positive breast cancer with liver and bone metastases. Pathology confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obesity

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Appendectomy (age 25)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: night sweats, intermittent nausea, unintentional weight loss, intermittent headaches. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 144/88, HR 89, RR 17, Temp 98.5F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 55.9 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 7.4, Hgb 13.4, Plt 100, Cr 1.07

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
58-year-old female with IV invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. initial oncology consultation.

1. Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Port placement scheduled
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Richard Okafor, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Cynthia Jones
MRN: patient_2d12d8b2603e
Date of Report: 2023-01-19
Specimen: Left breast (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 5.9 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  ERBB2 Amplification (copy number 12) - Amplification [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PIK3CA p.H1047R - Missense (Exon 20)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CHEK2 p.I157T - VUS
  PALB2 p.L939W - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - ERBB2 amplification: FDA-approved HER2-directed therapies include trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DXd, tucatinib.
  - PIK3CA H1047R: Alpelisib may be considered in HR+/HER2- setting.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 567x coverage.
Tumor content: 38%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Cynthia Jones
MRN: patient_2d12d8b2603e
Date of Study: 2023-05-04

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. restaging after first-line chemotherapy with dual HER2 blockade.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.8 cm in the left lower lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: 3 hypodense lesions consistent with metastases, largest in segment 6 measuring 3.2 cm. New finding.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Multiple sclerotic osseous lesions involving pelvis and bilateral iliac bones consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

Patient: Cynthia Jones
MRN: patient_2d12d8b2603e
Date of Study: 2023-11-18

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. restaging after second-line HER2-directed ADC.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.8 cm in the left upper lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: 2 hypodense lesions consistent with metastases, largest in segment 6 measuring 2.1 cm. New finding.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Multiple sclerotic osseous lesions involving lumbar spine, pelvis, and bilateral proximal femora consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.

Department of Radiology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Cynthia Jones
MRN: patient_2d12d8b2603e
Date of Study: 2024-09-02

CLINICAL INDICATION: Invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 2.0 cm in the right lower lobe. Stable in size.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: 3 hypodense lesions consistent with metastases, largest in segment 4a measuring 2.5 cm. Decreased in size.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: Multiple mixed lytic-sclerotic osseous lesions involving pelvis and bilateral iliac bones consistent with osseous metastatic disease.

IMPRESSION:
1. Known breast malignancy with hepatic and osseous metastases.
2. Liver lesions decreased in size, partial response.
3. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

scenario_blurb: Stage IV HER2-positive breast cancer with liver and bone metastases
